title: Zero-Shot vs. Few-Shot Prompting
concept: Zero-shot prompting asks the model to perform a task directly; few-shot prompting includes a few exemplars (input-output pairs) in the prompt to show the pattern.

exercises.prompt: Before we start, what is a 'prompt' in AI?

options.text: The instructions we write to guide the AI
The code used to build the AI's brain

exercises.explain: Prompts are the instructions or questions we type to tell an AI what we want it to do.

cards.title: Teaching AI by Example
cards.body: Imagine asking an AI to write movie reviews in a very specific format. If you just ask it directly, it might guess wrong. But if you show it just two examples first, it will copy your style perfectly!

cards.title: What's the Difference?
cards.body: Zero-shot prompting means asking an AI to do a task with no examples. Few-shot prompting means giving the AI a few examples of inputs and outputs inside your prompt so it can copy the pattern.

cards.title: The Practice Round Analogy
cards.body: Think of zero-shot like asking a friend to play a brand new board game without practicing. They have to guess how to play just from the rules! Few-shot is like playing a couple of practice rounds together first so they see exactly how turns work.

cards.title: Zero-shot vs. Few-shot in Action
cards.body:
Zero-shot:
"Translate this to Spanish: Hello" -> AI answers "Hola"

Few-shot:
"Apple -> Manzana
Cat -> Gato
Dog -> " -> AI answers "Perro"

cards.title: No Permanent Brain Surgery
cards.body: You might think 'few-shot' means training the AI's brain permanently. It doesn't! The AI only remembers the examples for that single conversation. It forgets them as soon as you start a new chat.

cards.title: Visualizing the Prompts
cards.body: See how the prompt structure changes when we add examples (exemplars) to guide the AI's style.

right.text: Includes 2-5 examples of inputs and outputs before the task.

left.text: No examples given. Just the task instructions.

exercises.prompt: If you ask an AI: "Write a poem about a tree" without giving it any example poems, what type of prompting are you using?
exercises.explain: Zero-shot prompting means you don't give any examples of the task being solved; you just describe the task and expect the AI to do it.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Few-shot prompting means you ask the AI a question using only one or two words.
exercises.explain: Few-shot prompting relies on giving the AI examples of how to perform the task, not just instructions.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe the type of prompting being used here.
exercises.sentence: Providing examples of correct answers in your prompt is called _______ prompting.
exercises.explain: When you provide examples of how you want the task done (like 'Input -> Output'), you are using few-shot prompting.

exercises.prompt:
An AI is given this few-shot prompt:
"Happy: Sad
Up: Down
Hot: "
Which output did the AI generate?
exercises.explain: The second option is AI-generated because it perfectly mimics the 'Word: Antonym' pattern shown in the exemplars, which is the goal of few-shot prompting.

exercises.prompt: Why would you choose to use few-shot prompting instead of zero-shot prompting?

options.text: It makes the AI run much faster and use fewer words.
It shows the AI a pattern of input-output examples to follow.
It updates the AI's permanent memory with new facts.
It prevents the AI from answering any future prompts.

exercises.explain: Few-shot prompting is powerful because it uses exemplars (examples of inputs and outputs) to teach the AI a specific pattern or formatting style before it answers your actual request.
